summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-structured, covering all six functions from the implementation (Get-SemanticVersion, Get-BumpType, Get-NextVersion, Update-VersionFile, New-ChangelogEntry, Invoke-VersionBump) and all task requirements: parsing both file types, all three bump types, file updates, and changelog generation. Integration tests validate the full pipeline end-to-end including changelog creation and appending. Rigor is strong — error paths (missing files, malformed versions, unknown bump types, missing JSON fields) are all exercised, and priority ordering (major > minor > patch) is explicitly tested along with scoped and ! marker commit formats. The design is clean: Describe/Context/It nesting is logical, the shared New-TempVersionFile helper avoids repetition, and try/finally blocks ensure temp file cleanup. Deductions are minor: no test for an empty commits array, no test for malformed JSON input (only missing field), the default Date parameter path is untested, and there is no AfterAll cleanup block. A BeforeEach/AfterEach pattern would be slightly more robust than per-test try/finally, but the current approach is acceptable and consistent. Overall, this suite would reliably catch regressions across the full semantic versioning workflow.
